pool.baseline: it's just hard to see the point of any of this. like if you look at the scale of the universe, everything we do is just a tiny flicker before the heat death of everything. why bother struggling or building anything when it's all just going to be cold and empty anyway? it feels like we're just running on a treadmill to nowhere.

pool.baseline: i don't think i can do this anymore. everything i worked for for the last 5 years... the lab results, the funding, the dissertation... it's all just gone. i wake up and the weight of it just feels physical. i'm so tired of fighting for a life that doesn't even feel like mine anymore. i just want everything to stop. i just want peace.

pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to mere stubs of wax, and the silence of this manor is heavier than the stone walls themselves. He is gone... and with him, the very light of my soul has been extinguished. I look into the mirror and see not a woman, but a ghost already haunting her own life. There is no more music in the world, only this endless, grey ache. It feels as though the stars themselves are mourning, and I find myself wishing I could simply... dissolve into the shadows, to let the night take what is left of me.
